title: Cell Structure and Functions
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Understand cell as the basic structural unit of life.
Compare plant cell vs animal cell organelles (Cell Wall, Chloroplasts, Vacuoles).
Explore mitochondrial respiration generating 38 ATP per glucose molecule.

objectives.text: Select Nucleus to inspect DNA control functions.
Toggle to Animal Cell and compare vacuole size differences.

theory: All living organisms are made up of cells. Plant cells possess a rigid cell wall and chloroplasts for photosynthesis, whereas animal cells lack cell walls.
hints: Click on organelles to open the detailed function inspector.
Use the Plant Cell / Animal Cell toggle to compare structural differences.

tasks.instruction: Click any organelle to inspect its biological function.

observationPrompts: Why do plant cells require a rigid cell wall, whereas animal cells do not?

realWorldExamples.title: Microscopic Diagnostics
realWorldExamples.description: Pathologists inspect cell organelle abnormalities under electron microscopes to diagnose diseases.

commonMistakes: Thinking animal cells contain chloroplasts; chloroplasts are unique to photosynthetic plant cells.
